preamble: The shift from [t] to [θ] is the second part of the first act of Grimm's Law.

examples.note: Begedkefat spirantization: post-vocalic /t/ → [θ] in Biblical Hebrew. The final tāv is [θ] in Tiberian vocalization.

Kabyle spirantization: lax /t/ in post-vocalic and intervocalic positions weakens to the dental fricative [θ]. The shift is part of the Northern Berber spirantization system affecting all lax stops; geminate /tt/ and post-nasal /nt/ are immune.

Middle Atlas Tamazight (Ayt Ndhir dialect area) exhibits the same spirantization rule as Kabyle: lax /t/ → [θ]. This pattern is consistent across northern Moroccan and Algerian Berber varieties but absent in Tuareg and Zenaga.

sources: Kossmann, M. G. & Stroomer, H. J. (1997). Berber Phonology. In Kaye (ed.), Phonologies of Asia and Africa. Eisenbrauns.
